factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with some growth and decline.

evidence.text: Revenue ton-miles decreased 1% during the second quarter of 2022 compared to 2021, driven by a 1% decrease in carloadings, while gross ton-miles increased 1%. Year-to-date, gross ton-miles and revenue ton-miles increased 5% and 4%, respectively, driven by a 1% increase in carloadings.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals from both quarterly and year-to-date data suggest no clear directional trend.

factors.summary: Pricing power is partially offsetting cost increases.

evidence.text: Our second quarter operating ratio of 60.2% deteriorated 5.1 points compared to 2021 and our year-to-date operating ratio of 59.8% deteriorated 2.3 points compared to 2021 mainly due to excess network costs, higher fuel prices, inflation, and other cost increases, partially offset by positive mix of traffic, and core pricing gains.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Pricing gains partially offset cost increases, indicating moderate positive impact.

evidence.text: Gross ton-miles and revenue ton-miles increased 5% and 4%, respectively, driven by a 1% increase in carloadings. Changes in commodity mix drove the variances in both periods between gross ton-miles, revenue ton-miles, and carloads.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear evidence of revenue growth driven by increased ton-miles and carloadings.

factors.summary: Volumes are declining due to network congestion.

evidence.text: Freight car velocity, average train speed, and average terminal dwell deteriorated, operating car inventory levels increased and congested the network compared to the same periods in 2021.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Network congestion is causing a decline in volumes, indicating a headwind.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly fuel prices and inflation.

evidence.text: Higher fuel prices, inflation, and other cost increases are impacting the operating ratio.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs, particularly fuel prices and inflation, pose a significant challenge.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to wage inflation and workforce expansion.

evidence.text: Workforce productivity declined 2% in the second quarter of 2022, as average daily car miles were essentially flat while employees increased 2% compared to 2021. The 2% increase in employee levels was driven by an increase in train, engine, and yard employees to address congestion and market demands.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to wage inflation and workforce expansion, impacting efficiency.

factors.summary: Margins are compressing due to higher costs and network congestion.

evidence.text: Our second quarter operating ratio of 60.2% deteriorated 5.1 points compared to 2021 and our year-to-date operating ratio of 59.8% deteriorated 2.3 points compared to 2021 mainly due to excess network costs, higher fuel prices, inflation, and other cost increases, partially offset by positive mix of traffic, and core pricing gains.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher costs and network congestion are compressing margins, indicating a negative trend.

factors.summary: Productivity is declining due to network congestion and workforce changes.

evidence.text: Workforce productivity declined 2% in the second quarter of 2022, as average daily car miles were essentially flat while employees increased 2% compared to 2021. Locomotive productivity decreased in the second quarter and six-month periods of 2022 compared to the same periods in 2021 driven by an increase in our average active fleet size as resources were deployed to alleviate network congestion in both periods and handle increased volume in the six-month period of 2022.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining productivity due to congestion and workforce changes is a significant concern.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is deteriorating due to network congestion.

evidence.text: Freight car velocity, average train speed, and average terminal dwell deteriorated, operating car inventory levels increased and congested the network compared to the same periods in 2021.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows clear signs of supply chain deterioration with network congestion impacting key metrics.

factors.summary: Management is showing cautious optimism through capital expenditures and share repurchases.

evidence.text: In 2022, we expect our capital expenditures to be approximately $3.3 billion, up 10% from 2021, as we make investments to support our growth strategy. We are also repurchasing shares under our share repurchase program.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased capital expenditures and share repurchases indicate cautious optimism and confidence in growth.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include network congestion, labor negotiations, and economic conditions.

evidence.text: Network congestion is impacting freight car velocity and terminal dwell times. Labor negotiations are ongoing, and economic conditions include higher fuel prices and inflation.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Multiple near-term risks such as network congestion, labor negotiations, and economic conditions pose challenges.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices, network congestion, and labor negotiations.

evidence.text: Higher fuel prices, inflation, and other cost increases are impacting the operating ratio. Network congestion is affecting freight car velocity and terminal dwell times. Labor negotiations are ongoing.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher fuel prices, network congestion, and labor negotiations are key sensitivities that could impact performance.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing to support growth.

evidence.text: In 2022, we expect our capital expenditures to be approximately $3.3 billion, up 10% from 2021, as we make investments to support our growth strategy.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased capital expenditures signal investment in growth, which is a positive development.

evidence.text: Adjusted debt increased from $31,416 million at December 31, 2021, to $33,503 million at June 30, 2022.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The increase in debt leverage could indicate potential financial strain, which is a negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained through dividends and share repurchases.

evidence.text: We paid dividends of $1.556 billion in the first six months of 2022 and repurchased $0.7 billion under our share repurchase program.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Maintaining shareholder returns through dividends and repurchases shows commitment to returning value, which is positive.

factors.summary: Free cash flow decreased due to higher capital investments.

evidence.text: Free cash flow decreased from $1,798 million in 2021 to $1,071 million in 2022 due to higher capital investments.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in free cash flow due to higher investments is a headwind, indicating a negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a headwind with inflation and supply chain issues.

evidence.text: Higher fuel prices, inflation, and other cost increases are impacting the operating ratio. Network congestion is affecting freight car velocity and terminal dwell times.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Inflation and supply chain issues are negatively impacting operations.

factors.summary: Headcount is increasing to address congestion and market demands.

evidence.text: Total employees increased by 2% in the second quarter of 2022 compared to 2021, driven by an increase in train, engine, and yard employees to address congestion and market demands.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Headcount increase indicates proactive addressing of congestion and market demands.

factors.summary: Labor relations are a risk with ongoing negotiations.

evidence.text: The current round of negotiations began on January 1, 2020, related to years 2020-2024. In June 2022, the National Mediation Board released the parties from mediation, which initiated the first 30-day cooling-off period.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Ongoing negotiations and a cooling-off period suggest potential labor risks.
